title: Pfizer Company Overview
text: Pfizer is a research-based global biopharmaceutical company headquartered in New York. The company discovers, develops, manufactures, markets, sells, and distributes prescription medicines and vaccines worldwide. Pfizer's portfolio includes products across oncology, vaccines, internal medicine, inflammation and immunology, anti-infectives, rare disease, and hospital care. Important products and franchises include Eliquis, Prevnar, Vyndaqel, Comirnaty, Ibrance, Paxlovid, Xtandi, Padcev, Nurtec ODT/Vydura, Xeljanz, and several oncology biosimilars. Pfizer also generates revenue from Pfizer CentreOne, a contract development and manufacturing business. For investment analysis, Pfizer is important because performance depends on product demand, new launches, patent and exclusivity timelines, clinical trial results, regulatory approvals, pricing pressure, Medicare and government drug policies, COVID-19 product seasonality, cost reduction programs, acquisitions such as Seagen and Metsera, and the company's ability to rebuild growth through its late-stage pipeline.
